sections.title: Narrative Overview: May 13–20, 2026
sections.content: This report covers one full week of studio activity across revenue, class attendance, and membership health. The sections below highlight daily trends, demand patterns, and membership plan distribution — each with a focused takeaway and actionable guidance. Use this as your weekly pulse check to catch early signals before they compound into larger issues.

sections.title: Daily Revenue Trend
sections.sql: SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND day >= '2026-05-13' AND day <= '2026-05-20' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
sections.content: Daily revenue fluctuations over a single week often reveal which days drive disproportionate income — typically tied to peak class schedules or promotional activity. Flat or dipping mid-week days are a common pattern in studios and represent an opportunity to introduce targeted offers or off-peak incentives. If a specific day significantly outperforms others, investigate whether that correlates with a popular instructor or class type to replicate the conditions.

sections.title: Daily Class Attendance (Check-Ins)
sections.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m-%d', start_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS visits FROM vw_class_attendance WHERE status = 'confirmed' AND checked_in_at IS NOT NULL AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', start_at) >= '2026-05-13' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', start_at) <= '2026-05-20' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
sections.content: Attendance volume across the week shows which days your floor is busiest and where capacity may be underutilized. Mismatches between high-attendance days and revenue (seen in the chart above) can indicate heavy membership redemption rather than pay-per-class bookings — worth monitoring for revenue mix balance. Low-attendance days are prime candidates for promotional class drops, workshop pilots, or instructor-led community events to build habit and fill capacity.

sections.title: Membership Plan Distribution
sections.sql: SELECT plan_name, COUNT(*) AS member_count FROM vw_memberships WHERE state = 'active' GROUP BY plan_name ORDER BY member_count DESC
sections.content: The distribution of active members across plans reveals which tiers your clients gravitate toward and where your recurring revenue base is concentrated. Over-reliance on a single low-cost plan can cap revenue growth even as headcount grows — a sign to consider upsell campaigns toward higher-tier or unlimited plans. If premium plans represent a small slice, consider a limited-time upgrade promotion or adding perks (guest passes, priority booking) to make the step-up feel worthwhile.

sections.title: Recommendations
sections.content: 1. **Boost mid-week revenue**: Identify the lowest-revenue days from the daily trend and schedule a flash promotion, drop-in special, or new class format to stimulate demand. 2. **Leverage peak attendance days**: Cross-reference your highest check-in days with revenue — if attendance is high but revenue isn't, introduce upsell touchpoints (retail, add-ons, workshops) at those peak moments. 3. **Drive membership upsells**: If lower-tier plans dominate the distribution, launch a targeted email campaign to members on entry plans highlighting the value of upgrading — even a 10–15% shift to a higher plan meaningfully improves MRR. 4. **Monitor weekly trends consistently**: One week is a snapshot; set a recurring weekly review cadence using this same report structure to spot trend changes early, especially around instructor availability and class schedule shifts.
